ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, MOTION FOR AN ORDER BARRING THE USE OF TESTIMONY OF MONTE MANWILL BY PLAINTIFF. THE COURT MADE THE FOLLOWING RULING: DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' MOTIONS BARRING PLAINTIFF'S USE OF THE DEPOSITION OF MONTE MANWILL ARE DENIED. DEFENDANTS' MOTIONS FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER ARE GRANTED. MONTE MANWILL'S DEPOSITION IN THE SUBLETT CASE WILL OCCUR ON MARCH 26. BY MARCH 17 PLAINTIFF WILL PROVIDE TO DEFENDANTS A LIST OF LOCATIONS AND DATES TO BE OFFERED BY MR. MANWILL DURING DEPOSITION. PURSUANT TO PLAINTIFF'S STIPULATION THE ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE (PREVIOUSLY FILED ON 2/25/15) IS MODIFIED. AS TO DEFENDANTS PETER KIEWIT, CHICAGO BRIDGE, TEMPORARY PLANT AND SEQUOIA VENTURES' SUMMARY MOTIONS, NOTICES CAN BE FURTHER SHORTENED TO 15 DAYS, WITH OPPOSITIONS DUE 11 DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING, AND REPLIES DUE 3 DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. THE THREE HEARING DATES OF SUMMARY MOTIONS WILL REMAIN THE SAME. DEFENDANT PETER KIEWIT SONS', INC. WILL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON. COURT REPORTER: EARLY K. LANGLEY, CSR #3537.

THE OBJECTIONS TO DR. EWING'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-29-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sean Patterson of SEDGWICK LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party Peter Kiewit Sons', Inc.
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Larry Sublett

RULING:
DEFENDANT PETER KIEWIT SONS', INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Prior the hearing the Court disclosed its working relationship with Counsel Michael L. Fox of the Sedgwick firm, details on the record. The Court found that this would not jeopardize its ability to rule in this hearing and did not recuse itself.
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Peter Kiewit Sons', Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Monte Manwill's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether the employees at issue were employed by defendant, the sole issue raised by defendant. Assuming that the declarations of Mr. Hindes and Mr. Hoffman are admissible, they merely create a triable issue. Defendant's objections to Mr. Manwill's declaration are overruled. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
In chambers the Court further ruled and signed an order re: defendant's evidentiary objections to the Declaration of Monte Manwill. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tamara Willat of WILLAT COURT REPORTING, CSR #4609 (Tel: 415-272-1753) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Intervenors
Off Calendar May-28-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sabrina L. Axt of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for intervenors / moving parties Maryland Casualty Company and Fireman's Fund Insurance Company on behalf of their insured, Marconi Plastering Company, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff
/ opposing party Larry Sublett

RULING:
INTERVENORS MARYLAND CASUALTY COMPANY AND FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY ON BEHALF OF THEIR INSURED, MARCONI PLASTERING COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Intervenors Maryland Casualty Co. and Fireman's Fund Insurance Co., on behalf of defendant Marconi Plastering Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied as to both prongs. The negation prong is denied, defendant did not meet the burden. As to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar the intervenors sustained their initial burden. Robert Cantley's deposition testimony and declaration and William Ewing's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (joint compound) attributable to Marconi. The objections to Dr. Ewing's declaration are overruled. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
In chambers, the Court further ruled and signed an order re: defendants' objection to evidence submitted by plaintiff in opposition to the motion for summary judgment. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Limbach Facility Services, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar May-20-2015 Off Calendar

Defendant Devcon Construction Incorporated'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar May-28-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christopher B. Bruni of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing for defendant / moving party Devcon Construction Incorporated
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Larry Sublett

RULING:
DEFENDANT DEVCON CONSTRUCTION INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Devcon Construction Incorporated's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Defendant's evidence - Bret Sisney deposition testimony and declaration, defendant's Articles of Incorporation, Dana Bennett's declaration and plaintiff's responses to other defendants' discovery - does not negate the testimony of Robert Cantley. Specifically, it does not demonstrate that defendant did not perform work at the Lockheed Sunnyvale campus in the mid-1970s nor does it demonstrate that the products/materials to which defendant's employees exposed asbestos did not contain asbestos. At most, when read together, this evidence merely creates a triable issue for the jury. Additionally, although it does not appear that defendant attempted to sustain its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar, if it had it would be precluded from doing so because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Robert Cantley's deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
